decks.notes: The premise of this deck revolves around quickly assembling the Phyrexian Dreadnought combo, which involves playing a 12/12 creature for only one mana by stacking it with Stifle's ability to counter the Dreadnought's "enters the battlefield" ability. The deck also includes various control elements such as Counterspell and Foil to protect the combo, as well as card draw and selection spells like Gush and Opt to find the necessary pieces. Vision Charm and Portent provide additional utility and card advantage, while Mishra's Factory offers a secondary win condition.

This deck focuses on quickly developing a board presence by using cards with the Madness mechanic to efficiently cast spells and creatures. The deck utilizes card draw and selection to ensure consistent plays, while also including disruptive elements like counterspells and creature removal to control the game. The mana base is streamlined to support the aggressive strategy, including key lands like Gaea's Cradle to generate extra mana. Ultimately, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a combination of aggression and disruption, taking advantage of the Madness mechanic to gain card advantage.

The main concept of this Enchantress combo deck is to generate a strong card advantage through enchantress effects such as Argothian Enchantress and Enchantress's Presence. By efficiently ramping with cards like Wild Growth and Exploration, the deck aims to quickly build up mana to cast powerful spells like Replenish and Opalescence. Sterling Grove provides protection and card selection, while cards like Parallax Wave and Swords to Plowshares offer removal options. The deck also includes various lock pieces like Elephant Grass and Solitary Confinement to control the board and buy time to assemble its combo pieces.

Thriving on speed and aggression, this Goblin deck aims to overwhelm opponents quickly and efficiently. By utilizing mana acceleration and tribal synergies, it can quickly flood the board with small but powerful creatures like Goblin Lackey and Goblin Piledriver. With support from cards like Gempalm Incinerator and Siege-Gang Commander, the deck can deal substantial damage to the opponent's life total in just a few turns. Overall, the key strategy is to apply constant pressure and maintain board presence to outpace the opponent and secure a swift victory.

This deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana with Elves to play powerful creatures like Deranged Hermit and Masticore. It also includes utility creatures like Wirewood Symbiote and Quirion Ranger to provide additional benefits. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with an aggressive strategy, while also having the option to control the board with cards like Tangle Wire. The inclusion of cards like Survival of the Fittest allows for consistency in finding key creatures, while Biorhythm can quickly end the game in the player's favor.

The main objective of this deck is to quickly build up resources in the graveyard to enable madness and threshold abilities, allowing for aggressive plays. It focuses on utilizing cards such as Wild Mongrel and Basking Rootwalla to discard cards and trigger madness, while also utilizing cards like Circular Logic to take advantage of having cards in the graveyard. Additionally, Survival of the Fittest is used to search for key creatures and set up the graveyard for threshold bonuses. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and efficient spells.

This Mono Black Control deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy through a variety of discard spells like Cabal Therapy and Duress, as well as removal spells like Smother and Innocent Blood. The deck also utilizes cards like Contamination and Engineered Plague to control the board and prevent the opponent from developing their own game plan. Additionally, the deck can generate card advantage with Skeletal Scrying and Zombie Infestation, while also having the ability to lock down the opponent's mana base with Wasteland and Rishadan Port.

This deck aims to stall and control the game using the Stasis enchantment to activate a lock on the opponent, preventing them from untapping their permanents. The combo aspect comes into play with cards like Black Vise and Brain Freeze, which can quickly shut down the opponent once the lock is in place. The deck focuses on card draw and countering opponent's spells to stay ahead while using Forsaken City and Gush for additional mana advantage. The win condition is to either deck out the opponent or deal enough direct damage to secure victory.

The foundation of this deck is centered around utilizing counterspells and removal spells to control the board, while also generating card advantage through draw spells like Accumulated Knowledge and Fact or Fiction. The creatures in the deck, such as Morphling and Mishra's Factory, provide versatile threats and ways to close out the game. With a mix of counterspells, removal, card draw, and threats, this deck aims to outlast and outmaneuver opponents in a grindy, attrition-based game plan.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by playing a defensive strategy focused on countering opponent's spells and removing threats with efficient removal spells like Swords to Plowshares. The deck aims to create card advantage through cards like Fact or Fiction and Standstill, while also utilizing powerful control cards such as Parallax Tide and Wrath of God to keep the board in check. By utilizing a mix of counterspells, removal, and card advantage, the deck aims to outmaneuver opponents and secure victory through incremental advantage.

This deck focuses on controlling the game through counterspells, removal, and disrupting the opponent's tempo. By utilizing powerful control cards like Counterspell, Mana Leak, and Swords to Plowshares, the deck aims to manage threats while gradually gaining card advantage through spells like Fact or Fiction and Accumulated Knowledge. The key strategy is to establish control of the board with cards like Powder Keg and Parallax Tide, while also generating value from lands like Mishra's Factory and Faerie Conclave. Overall, the deck aims to outlast the opponent and win through attrition.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize the Oath of Druids card to quickly get Terravore onto the battlefield, while controlling the opponent's resources with removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Cataclysm. The deck also includes some card draw and recursion with Deep Analysis and Quiet Speculation. Additionally, it features some disruption with cards like Aura of Silence and Naturalize, while maintaining a solid mana base with various lands like Gemstone Mine and Rishadan Port. Overall, the deck aims to control the game while ramping up to powerful creatures like Terravore.

The focus of this deck is to control the game by using counterspells and removal spells to disrupt the opponent's strategy while building card advantage through cantrips and draw spells. The main win condition is to use Psychatog's ability to grow in power based on the number of cards in your graveyard, while also using Wonder as a backup option for evasion. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent and outmaneuver them with efficient spells and timely disruption.

This deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with enchantments like Opalescence and Parallax Wave, then using Replenish to bring them all back onto the battlefield at once for a powerful combo. Using cards like Intuition and Attunement to set up the combo and control spells like Swords to Plowshares and Seal of Cleansing to disrupt the opponent, it aims to overwhelm the opponent with a massive army of enchantments. The key strategy is to carefully manage resources and timing to execute the combo effectively and secure a win.

The deck utilizes cards like Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to disrupt opponents' strategies and set up a game-winning combo. By efficiently countering spells and drawing cards with Gush and Foil, the deck aims to quickly assemble the combo while protecting it with cards like Counterspell. The inclusion of cards like Portent and Vision Charm helps to dig deeper into the deck for key pieces, while Mishra's Factory adds a creature threat. The deck's strategy revolves around assembling the powerful combo and disrupting opponents' plans to secure a victory.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize the combo of Phyrexian Devourer and Altar of Dementia to mill through cards in order to deal a lethal amount of damage with Fling. The deck also includes cards like Tinker and Oath of Druids to help find key combo pieces quickly. Defense Grid helps protect the combo from disruption, while mana acceleration sources like Lotus Petal and Mox Diamond enable fast, explosive plays. Overall, the deck aims to assemble its combo pieces efficiently and win the game in a single turn.
